title: Hippeastrum cyrtanthoides
taxonomy: Hippeastrum cyrtanthoides is a species of flowering plant in the family Amaryllidaceae. It is native to southern Africa and is often referred to as a type of amaryllis. Within the Amaryllidaceae family, Hippeastrum cyrtanthoides is part of the subfamily Amaryllidoideae. This classification is also known as the amaryllid clade, which includes various other bulbous flowering plants. The classification of Hippeastrum cyrtanthoides reflects its unique characteristics and evolutionary history within the Amaryllidaceae family.

intro: Hippeastrum cyrtanthoides, also known as the Amaryllis belladonna, is a flowering plant species native to South Africa, belonging to the Amaryllidaceae family. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its various health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help alleviate respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as wound healing and skin conditions. The most common herbal preparations made with this plant include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are used to treat a range of ailments, including respiratory and skin conditions, and as a natural remedy for fever reduction.
